videos.title: MoE live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
videos.description:
A steamroller of sound, that is MoE. Frontwoman Guro Skumsnes Moe on the microphone and bass – a true legend of the punk/noise underground scene in Norway and beyond – has performed for the first time ever in Berlin with her longtime partner Håvard Skaset on the guitar. Special guests include drummer Veslemøy Narvesen and Mette Rasmussen on the saxophone. With this illustrious new line-up, the group will continue to work within the unwavering spirit of their own berserker creativity. According to MoE, you could call it “rock” if absolutely necessary, but really it’s so much more than that. The deck has been reshuffled, anything is possible.

MoE live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
GURO MOE (NO) — ELECTRIC BASS, VOCALS

videos.description:
DARA String Festival presents a wide range of innovative, genre-bending music for string instruments, ranging from early contemporary music to experimental

LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Grégoire Simon - Viola, Electronics, Percussion

Grégoire Simon works between Berlin and Paris. After studying violin and viola he was chosen by Pierre Boulez to take the solo viola position in the prestigious Ensemble Intercontemporain (Paris). Half of the electronic duo Yes Soeur! Grégoire Simon has been producing original music for visual installations and contemporary dance shows. With his Berlin based project SUGAR, Grégoire Simon built bridges between chamber music and pop music with original creations. 
Simon obtained various composition commissions played by the Ensemble Flashback (Perpignan), Solistenensemble Kaleidoskop, Zafraan Ensemble and Andromeda Mega Express Orchestra.

videos.title: DEFNE ŞAHİN @ Zig Zag Jazz Club | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Born, raised and based in Berlin, composer and songwriter Defne Şahin has established herself in the international music scene with her unique, cosmopolitan sound that seamlessly blends Jazz, Pop and Turkish music. As the daughter of Turkish immigrants and influenced by her experiences living between New York, Barcelona and Istanbul, Defne Şahin is a shapeshifter who connects multiple worlds and cultures through her music. She sets herself apart with her versatile and eclectic storytelling rooted in her multicultural background, allowing her to effortlessly bridge genres. Her new album Hope (October 2023) is an enchanting song cycle of Emily Dickinson’s poetry paired with her own lyrics. It reflects on universal themes such as life and death, love and longing, fear and hope and the letting go of it all. 

DEFNE ŞAHİN @ Zig Zag Jazz Club, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer

videos.title: JOY GUIDRY live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2023
videos.description:
Rarely before has a bassoon been played in with such a rich diversity as in the hands and on the lips of Joy Guidry. The improviser, composer, storyteller and activist takes her music to both ambient abysses and paradisiacal pastures. Guidry transports the classic lyrical qualities of the instrument into atonal outbursts and political rebellion. Süddeutsche Zeitung describes Guidry’s latest album Radical Acceptance as one of “the strongest statements that free music has made recently.” On the stage, this diversity of sound is accompanied by a highly sensitive six-person ensemble that takes the counterplay of tension and redemption to an entirely new dimension. A L’ARME! brought the live performance of Radical Acceptance to Berlin for its world premiere. 

JOY GUIDRY @ A L'ARME! Festival 2023, Radialsystem, Berlin | 2023
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

DARA String Festival presents a wide range of innovative, genre-bending music for string instruments, ranging from early contemporary music to experimental

Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media, and INM Berlin.

Performer
Biliana Voutchkova - Violin
Grégoire Simon - Viola
Zoé Cartier - Cello

More about CARTIER - SIMON - VOUTCHKOVA:

Biliana Voutchkova is a dynamic, thoroughly engaged interdisciplinary artist, violinist, composer-performer, improvisor and curator with highly individual, unconventional artistic language. Her work spans the widest possible range of sound, vision, movement and includes concert performances of contemporary composition and original site specific work, exhibitions, long durational / multidisciplinary performances. She is based between Berlin and her rural residency on the black sea coast of Bulgaria.
http://www.bilianavoutchkova.net/

Grégoire Simon works between Berlin and Paris. After studying violin and viola he was chosen by Pierre Boulez to take the solo viola position in the prestigious Ensemble Intercontemporain (Paris). Half of the electronic duo Yes Soeur! Grégoire Simon has been producing original music for visual installations and contemporary dance shows. With his Berlin based project SUGAR, Grégoire Simon built bridges between chamber music and pop music with original creations. 
Simon obtained various composition commissions played by the Ensemble Flashback (Perpignan), Solistenensemble Kaleidoskop, Zafraan Ensemble and Andromeda Mega Express Orchestra.
https://www.gregoiresimon.com/

Zoé Cartier's key qualities are her high musical sensitivity and her uncompromising search for artistic expression. After successfully completing her solo studies at the Hanns Eisler Academy of Music in Berlin, the french musician was engaged as principal cellist at the Magdeburg Opera House. She has performed with various ensembles, including the Kammerakademie Potsdam, the Ensemble Resonanz, Solistenensemble Kaleidoskop and working with musicians from Ensemble Adapter, Zaffraan Ensemble, K.N.M Berlin, Vokalsolisten Stuttgart, Zeitgenössische Oper Berlin and is a permanent member of Ensemble LUX:NM. Her openness and curiosity open up a wide range of possibilities for her collaborations with artists such as Tino Segal, the choreographer Sacha Waltz and the theater director Falk Richter.

videos.title: DEADEYE feat. OTIS SANDSJÖ @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Three of Europe’s most forward thinking jazz musicians join forces with Deadeye, a brand new jazz supergroup. Deadeye uses the tried and tested formula of Hammond organ, drums and guitar to shed new light on the wildly diverse influences of its members. Although all three of its members share a love for improvisation and the unexpected, they each bring something deeply personal to the table: ethereal textures, undulating yet highly danceable rhythms and angular melodies.

LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: LORDS OF LOUNGE @ Orania | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Dad-rock, surf-pop, Meter-esque riffs, indie-strumming…all included. 
Four Berlin-based musicians formed Lords of Lounge to channel some surf-inspired, easy-listening lounge classics. Their method is simple; Recording everything while composing. No demos. No overdubs. Pure vibes and inspiration straight to tape.
Hard to believe but this was their first gig ever, celebrating the launch of their debut self-titled album. 

LORDS OF LOUNGE - live @ ORANIA, BERLIN | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

MACH (DO) is the title of a cycle of pieces for solo instruments which Eres Holz began in 2011 with a virtuoso trumpet solo and which now comprises nine pieces, with live electronics added since the clarinet piece of 2016. MACH! is both an exhortation and an homage to the physicist Ernst Mach. The unit of measurement that bears  the latter’s name is still used to indicate the speed of supersonic aircraft – a suggestion of the diffculty of the pieces. MACH, as written in the score of the organ piece, is “a kind of invitation to do that which is not trivial

videos.title: Joanna Mattrey @ DARA String Festival - Theater im Delphi | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
DARA String Festival presents a wide range of innovative, genre-bending music for string instruments, ranging from early contemporary music to experimental
and freely improvised music. DARA transcends genre boundaries, bringing together high-caliber international artists from diverse musical backgrounds in a series of encounters to stimulate new thoughts and ideas for the advancement of individual and collective artistic endeavors.

Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media, and INM Berlin. 
Gefördert vom Musikfonds e.V. mit Projektmitteln der Beauftragten der Bundesregierung für Kultur und Medien, und INM Berlin. 

Joanna Mattrey - live @ Theater im Delphi, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer:
Joanna Mattrey - Viola

Joanna is a violist working in free improvisation, new music, and classical music. She uses extended techniques, modern compositional approaches, and electronic alterations to challenge the conventions of the viola. Joanna creates an embodied performance practice centered on ceremony and ritual. Mattrey has performed with icons Tyshawn Sorey, Henry Threadgill, Marc Ribot, John Zorn, Billy Martin, Elliott Sharpe, and the International Contemporary Ensemble.
https://www.joannamattrey.com

videos.title: SKULTURA @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Nick Dunston is a Berlin based composer and performer. His work touches on ideas such as embodiment, ancestral memory, and Afrosurrealism. On Skultura, he continuously shapes the music with various sound-manipulation techniques to create an album that is meticulous as it is visceral. In addition to his broad solo practice, he also constantly engages with improvisers and artists of other mediums as a means of cultivating an inquisitive, experimental, and beautiful body of collaborative works.

LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: OĞUZ BÜYÜKBERBER @ Pantopia Festival / Spreehalle | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Oğuz Büyükberber’s PROCESSING aims to challenge the listener’s sensory perception. What is
acoustic, what is synthesized, what is distorted or altered?
Reflecting on his own distorted and very limited sight, Büyükberber layers gesture over gesture, texture over texture and idea over idea in this hour-long, ever-evolving, intense piece of music full of juxtaposed references. Works by Max Ernst, Cy Twobly, Pierre Boulez, Kraftwerk, Bootsy Collins, Wendy Carlos, Alfred Hitchcock, Mauricio Kagel and, of course, John Cage, as well as Oğuz’s own personal history, dreams and nightmares form the basis of what inspires the eclectic sound novel that is this work. Between the lines, or sometimes in your face, are elements linked to pop culture, comic books, science fiction, psychoanalysis, architecture and family drama. 

OĞUZ BÜYÜKBERBER - live @ Spreehalle, Berlin | 2023
As part of Pantopia Festival
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Performer
Oğuz Büyükberber - Clarinet, Modular Synthesizer, Ableton Push, Live Drawing

videos.title: SCHULZ UND SÖHNE @ Spreehalle / Altes Kraftwerk, Pantopia Festival | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Handmade, improvised rhythms to develop precise beats that merge into a kind of “analog techno" performed with a wild collection of percussion instruments made from scrap and discarded everyday objects: escalator steps, steel springs, canisters and coconuts. Schulz and Söhne appears exclusively as a live formation and has established itself as a permanent fixture in the Berlin music scene, is a regular guest at national and international music festivals such as Fusion, Melt, Garbicz and others.

videos.description:
Anger and vulnerability are closely related in Alexis Hountondji's project Kofi. With his powerful sound he has already conquered international stages and festivals in France, Belgium and Senegal. videos.title: Max Andrzejewski's Hütte @ Bonello Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Beautiful, brittle, guitar based pieces, a new calm, a fire underneath. This band started its journey twelve years ago, released four albums, won the Neuer Deutscher Jazzpreis in 2013 and literally toured the world. Enjoying reduction, repetition and serious playfulness, the search for the 
material´s essence, purely following emotional musical decisions, not exuberant concepts. 
Max´ extensive work as a composer in the field of contemporary music over the last years influenced the way he shaped the bands new music -  but in a different way one might think.

FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin

videos.title: SERA KALO'S EXANTE @ Festsaal Kreuzberg | XJAZZ! Festival | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
eXante is a collection of genre-fluid songs characterized by passionately melodic avant-soul melodies that merge with a sultry atmosphere and unconventional rhythms - a window into the many universes within Sera Kalo’s person and artistry through sensitive song arrangements and poetic story-telling. Sera Kalo is a genre-fluid, Berlin-based Caribbean-American singer & songwriter. Sera's music serves as a powerful outlet for her unfiltered thoughts and raw energy. Her music is a reflection of who she is, and she uses it to express the many flavors of her identity. Through her music, she seeks to inspire and empower others to embrace their authentic selves.

videos.title: SANTROFI & THE OMNIVERSAL EARKESTRA feat. AK Yeboah @ Kesselhaus | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
SANTROFI is a dynamic collective of celebrated young musicians from Accra’s music scene, gathered together by bassist and producer, Emmanuel Ofori, known for his work with Highlife legend, Ebo Taylor and Pat Thomas’s Kwashibu Area Band. And THE OMNIVERSAL EARKESTRA is an unusual big band as well. There is no bandleader, the musical direction changes constantly. All members are brought together by their love for the classic big band sound. Together they celebrate their understanding of Highlife and Afro-Beat music with a big band sound. This has been a unique concert.

videos.title: HEAR NOW BERLIN @ Heimathafen, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
HEAR NOW BERLIN is a virtuosic, post-genre chamber sextet, creating original music and playing works by some of today's most cutting-edge composers. Merging contemporary classical, avant-garde, jazz and pop, they create the chamber music of now. In their concert they have compiled a series of compositions that illuminate various aspects of human life, creating an immersive musical journey. Excerpts from a concert with music by US composers Judd Greenstein and Nathan Schramm and a new piece by flutist Kelly Watson Woelffer.

videos.title: WITCH 'N' MONK @ FLUID FORM CLUB #2 | ausland, Berlin | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
With a touch of Frank Zappa lyricism and a radical punk aesthetic, the duo of Heidi Heidelberg and Mauricio Velasierra crafts a dramatically bizarre vocal music from another dimension! Dreamlike improvisations are sampled, manipulated and reinvented through the lens of contemporary music and pop. You have never heard anything quite like Witch ’n’ Monk!" (John Zorn) 
Radically electrified Latin American flutes with jazz-punk guitar/bass and anarchic soprano vocals. Creating contrast is fundamental to their work and thus they tread upon the edges of genres. Their latest album was named Contemporary Album of the Month by The Guardian and received the prestigious Preis der deutschen Schallplattenkritik.

Fluid Form Club presents passionately unpredictable music that escapes classification, for a community of curious minds.
Funded by Musikfonds e.V. by means of the Federal Government Commissioner for Culture and the Media

videos.title: Christoph Möckel Trio - live @ Bonello Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
An exciting electric trio in the rare line-up of bass-clarinet/saxophone, electric bass and drums. The extensive use of electronic live effects allows for an almost limitless sound exploration and leads to a homogeneous and surprising fusion that ranges from ambient to free to experimental jazz(rock). According to Deutschlandfunk, Christoph Möckel, one of the "most promising young German saxophonists". Together with Oliver Lutz and Moritz Baumgärtner they represent a new generation of young innovative jazz musicians from Berlin and Cologne. They allow imaginative soundscapes to flourish in highly energetic improvisations and interactions. Creative jazz on the pulse of time!

videos.description:
Bobby and Christopher met up for an Espresso Doppio in Kreuzberg to catch up, hadn´t seen each other for a while. Their conversation flowed through digital and analog worlds, jazz and hip hop beats, composition and improvisational concepts, celebrating the sound of young Berlin, and led the audience through deep bass valleys, through pulsing groove-scapes and the urban summer afternoon. videos.title: ERNTE - live @ Chez Chérie Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
The harvest is in! Five jazz musicians dedicate themselves to a repertoire of the 20th centur, harvest of their (crisis) time: resistance songs, workers' songs, partisan songs and anti-fascist fight songs. That is why, true to the motto "he who sows the wind will reap the storm", ERNTE plays music with and in protest. Without lyrics, but with great joy of playing and everything that jazz has to offer for this: Improvisation, unpredictability, energy, transcendence and a trace of melancholy.

FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: RAMEEN SAQIZADA @ Industriesalon Schöneweide | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Rubab, also known as Rabab is one of the national musical instruments of Afghanistan. It is also known as ‘the lion of instruments’. This instrument is made with a single piece of wood from the mulberry tree. This melodious instrument is immensely famous in the area of Afghanistan, Pakistan, Kashmir, and a few regions in north India. It is vastly used in the traditional musical forms of the regions. It is a proven entertaining instrument in all these places.
Ustad Rameen Saqizada is one of the profound masters of Rubab.

Rameen Saqizada - live @ Industriesalon Schöneweide, Berlin | 2023
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.
This piece has been performed within "Reinventing Kabul" - watch more here: https://youtu.be/ZVxosBs9cXE

videos.title: DUMAMA @ Aeden | XJAZZ! Festival | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
D U M A M A is concerned with the ways in which new forms can organically emerge when collaborations can center in intimacy and openness. As a compelling storyteller and lyricist, she works to seamlessly mend together the divide between traditional South African oral culture and futuristic globally-oriented poetics, with a delicate care and awareness of the value of both. Gugulethu Duma (aka D U M A M A) involves consciously deconstructing and critiquing archaic modes of representation of Southern African/African sonic and performance culture.

videos.title: TRAINING with RUTH GOLLER @ Factory Studio, Berlin | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
TRAINING played at Moers Festival, at Jazzfest Berlin (in transatlantic cooperation with John Dieterich - from Deerhoof), at Eurojazz Mexico, Saalfelden Jazz Festival, as well as numerous club tours. Furthermore they created a concert series with a wide variety of guests in Berlin called Group TRAINING. Ruth Goller is a London-based bassist who has been involved in the development of many British experimental projects. Her musical focus has been in recent years on the development of her own project "SKYLLA", in which many of her influences get together. 
Ruth's style of composing includes microtonal bass harmonics with layers of vocal harmonies.
The three create a new, energetic music collectively: Meandering grooves, complex instrumental-vocal nestings, expressive training units, weightless unison melodies.

TRAINING with Ruth Goller - live @ Factory Studio, Berlin | 2023
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: ANNA HAUSS @ Factory Studio | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Acoustic indie soul that touches everyone who has a soft spot for profound songs, based on extremely tasteful and reduced groove by bass, drums and her guitar. After studying Jazz at Leipzig conservatory and touring internationally as the front-woman for the band Still in the Woods, she also co-composed and performed a song for the Netflix series “The Queen's Gambit", which gained her fans around the globe. videos.description:
These four musicians play– in the best sense of the word - with classical and spiritual music from Korea, Jazz, Samulnori and contemporary improvised music. videos.description:
Exploring new musical aesthetics, the internationally acclaimed composer trio Dell-Lillinger-Westergaard walks the line between contemporary, experimental and Avantgarde music.

videos.title: Brigade Futur III & Spielvereinigung Sued @ OSTEN Festival | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Brigade Futur 3 takes art back to the street, to the Späti, to the playground, to the kitchen, to the balcony. Jazz, which finally concerns everyone again - and despite this, or perhaps because of it, is allowed to have and make murderous fun of itself.
If it doesn't work, it doesn't exist, and if it doesn't, it doesn't work. Free jazz in the original sense of the word.
Wolf Kampmann

This is how political music of the present – provocative, catchy, subversive and driving. Beautiful and reflecting texts along with music that supports the principle of hope. This is an ambiguous declaration of war on our comfortable habits as a Dada, Swing, Rock and Jazz offensive. You rub your ears and then walk through our neoliberal perverted, cannibalistic consumer world with sharpened senses.
Ulrich Steinmetzger

Brigade Futur III & Spielvereinigung Sued - live @ OSTEN Festival, Bitterfeld | 2022
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: THE PRESENT - "Schwebstoff" @ Villa Elisabeth | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The piece "Schwebstoff" (floating matter) is being performed by nine voices of which one is dedicated to the spoken word, eight voices are singing. The original lyrics and music deal with our atmosphere as a global player, as fragile as powerful. A plot evolves around the character of an 'aerosole manager'. She knows how to separate vocal sounds from aerosoles and facilitates the public performance of a piece about the last survivors of an air-fallout. While the ensemble is figuring out the relationship of vocal music to the sky's humor, she slowly turns into the last aeronaut, floating in a vehicle that is lighter than air. 

THE PRESENT Vokalensemble: 
Olivia Stahn, Hanna Herfurtner, Stelina Apostolopoulou, Bernadette Beckermann, Amélie Saadia, Robert Sellier, William Frost, Elias Arranz, Florian Hille.

Funded by Hauptstadtkulturfonds
Thanks to: initiative neue musik, Foerderband, Adrian Holder / Wave Akademie, 
Musikfonds, Salon am Moritzplatz

videos.title: MOOR MOTHER & BAND presents JAZZ CODES @ Silent Green, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2022
videos.description:
Moor Mother is a cross between poet and cyborg, musician and mystic, activist and time traveler, who takes memories of injustice and pain and uses them to etch the idea of a different future onto our consciousness – and, on the way, finds sounds that we won’t soon forget. Jazz Codes represents the second part of a trilogy that issued an unmistakable call for an awakening in part one, Black Encyclopedia of the Air. A journey through outer space and the realization that enlightenment can sometimes be extremely dark.

MOOR MOTHER & BAND presents JAZZ CODES - live @ Silent Green Berlin | 2022

videos.title: TOBIAS CHRISTL WILDERN @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
With an audible joy in deconstructing, tearing down and rebuilding, truly new music is created in this jazz-quartet setting; music rich in rough edges, contrasts, breaks and unexpected turns. 
Some might say it is truly laid open. 'Wildern' is a foray through the dense underbrush of four decades of pop music, through songs from greats like Leonard Cohen, A-HA or Lana Del Rey.  
All the while, the soul of the songs is preserved.  

TOBIAS CHRISTL WILDERN @ House of Music, Berlin | 2022
LIVE FROM BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: ENSEMBLE DU @ St. Elisabeth Kirche | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
A subtle and highly intimate dialogue and interaction of Pia Davilas and Almut Kühnes soprano voices with Tilo Weber premiering on vibraphone. Inspired by and reflecting Rainer Maria Rilke's poem „Come, You“ and Martin Buber's book „I and Thou“ this fragile performance will easily give anybody the pleasure of a deep listening experience who is willing to open up to its beautiful and still substantial simplicity.

Ensemble Du - live @ St. Elisabeth Kirche, Berlin | 2022
LIVE IN BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Supported by Senate Department for Culture and Europe of Berlin.

videos.title: HYPNAGOGIA @ Atelier Wolfgang Flad | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
HYPNAGOGIA - the transition between wakefulness and sleep, control and release. 
Mbira and Maqam tonality meet African Jazz. An encounter between classical European instruments, bass clarinet, trumpet, flugelhorn and mbira, a traditional instrument from Southern Africa. Together they weave a unique new musical texture, beautifully relaxed and loaded with on point musicality. Lively warmth and shiny metallic buzz.

HYPNAGOGIA - live @ Atelier Wolfgang Flad, Berlin | 2022
LIVE IN BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: KEE AVIL - live @  Silent Green, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2022
videos.description:
This unique sound evolves out of the interplay of guitars with the clanging of broken tubs or the screech of metal screws on glass – all coupled, naturally, with the sound of her voice. Kee Avil creates song sculptures that are on the verge of collapse, and yet are consistently able to pull surprising grooves out of this precarious balance, perpetually drawing the listener in deeper.

videos.description:
Improvisation with an attitude. Maria Reich, the violinist from Berlin, and the singer/performer, Laura Totenhagen from Cologne, move in a trance-like state between the worlds of jazz, modern classical, and performance art, lyric poetry and feminist activism. Collaborating here for the first time with the French pianist and composer Eve Risser, who regularly transforms her piano into a percussive and harmonious treasure chest.

videos.title: KOMA SAXO feat. SOFIA JERNBERG @ JAZZEXZESS / House of Music | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
A highly potent lineup of five heavy jazz names on the Berlin–Nordics axis, including Eldh on bass, Christian Lillinger on drums and the frontline of three saxes: Otis Sandsjö, Jonas Kullhammar and Mikko Innanen. Here also featuring Sofia Jernberg on voice! Make no mistake: this is not just another "supergroup", but a real band with their own sound and musical trajectory and Eldh's vision for the Koma Saxo sound is one step ahead of what groovy avantgarde jazz could sound like.

KOMA SAXO feat. SOFIA JERNBERG @ House of Music, Berlin | 2022

videos.title: Field + Lena Czerniawska @ Studio Börne | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Having spent a few years together in this line-up and having released their first record together in 2021, FIELD now  further explores their layered weaving of contrapuntal lines, textures and rhythms with new works presented in this video. Integrated with and juxtaposed to the music are the live drawings and light projections by Lena Czerniawska, adding their own distinct dynamic to each of the three pieces and influence the sonic proceedings by visual means. With instant miniatures serving as book ends and interludes, the result has a poetry and poise of its own. 

Field + Lena Czerniawska - live @ Studio Börne | 2022

videos.title: Nils Wogram "Muse" - live @ Sendesaal, Bremen | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Nils Wogram's music has always had a great deal of sensuality, and in this project that means all the more: All or nothing! Sound is the key component. Trombone, saxophone, harp, viola and voice are the basis for this. The visionary, Nils Wogram, who never just searches for the sake of searching, but who makes the impossible possible and the unheard of, without any compromise.
The essence is the great sensitivity with which the musicians listen to each other, how they react and interact to the sounds they create together. 
This music is lyrical and full of poetry. Some kind kind of improvised chamber music with an affinity to film, contemporary classical, folk and jazz.
Deutscher Jazzpreis/German Jazz-Award 2022 - Album Instrumental

Nils Wogram "Muse" - live @ Sendesaal, Bremen | 2021
Filmed By Ear is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Günter "Baby" Sommer & Percussion Staff - live @ Festungsturm | JAZZWERKSTATT PEITZ
videos.description:
This extraordinary percussion ensemble around Francesco D'Auria and Günter "Baby" Sommer have been around for 35 years: The magical world of percussion, the ongoing search for new sounds from the instruments, the somnambulistic connection between the musicians - all this makes Percussion Staff so unique. The song of the elements of drum skins, wood, metal, water is joined by the melodic sound of marimba and vibraphone, trumpet and flugelhorn, whistle and shell and human voice, combine to form music that smells of the sea and olives, in which one can Hear the buzzing of the crickets.

videos.title: Stefan Schultze Large Ensemble "The Buchla Suite" - Rehearsal | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
The Buchla Suite: A Handcrafted Tribute to Morton Subotnick is the latest project and sound-exploration vehicle for Stefan Schultze’s Large Ensemble. The project was inspired by Morton Subotnick’s seminal composition Silver Apples of the Moon, which was released in 1967on Nonesuch Records. The piece was written for the Buchla-100 synthesizer, which Subotnick designed in collaboration with engineer Don Buchla. The introduction of this instrument, and Subotnick’s compositions which utilised it, defined what is now considered the West-Coast style of sound synthesis. His work has had a lasting impact on generations of electronic and electroacoustic musicians.

Stefan Schultze Large Ensemble "The Buchla Suite" - Rehearsal | 2022
Live from Berlin is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Peter Zegveld & Terrie Ex - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival
videos.description:
When the pot boils over, the feedback wails and the tea kettle whistles, it’s clear: Peter Zegveld and Terrie Ex are about to shock your system. The Dutch duo transform former industrial production lines into an adventurous audio playground. Musical craftsmanship becomes post-workerist expressionism. While performance artist Zegveld toils in his sound machine theatre like a deranged puppeteer, Terrie Hessels takes all of the power of deformation and the art of distortion that earned him the honorific Terrie Ex during his many years with The Ex and unleashes it full force on the guitar. Jazz that you can really let off steam to – does that even still exist? It certainly does. And, with Zegveld & Ex, you can take that literally.

Peter Zegveld & Terrie Ex - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

Terrie Ex - electric guitar

videos.title: The Killing Popes - live @ House of Music, Berlin | BITCHES BREW FESTIVAL
videos.description:
Disjointed structures, jumping grooves, a jazz statement for the second decade of the 21st century, introjecting aspects of art rock and electronic club music. It’s funny, it’s mind blowing, it’s still new and different, it’s the real equivalent of these so weird present times, but also an invitation to go ahead and party again, even if any body contortions you make with it hardly can be called dance. The old new jazz doesn’t have to die to give place to the new new one.

videos.title: Christian Kögel "Queen Jazz" (Part 2)  @ Zentrifuge | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Christian Kögel translates the music from Queen's "Jazz" album, which is rather not Jazz, into an energetic contemporary Berlin Jazz sound - with an electric baritone guitar instead of an electric bass and two tenor saxophones in an archaic, acoustically-oriented guise. The musicians' second instruments (oud, banjo, dobro, baritone saxophone, clarinet, bass clarinet) take up the very different facets of the original music.

videos.title: Karkhana - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2021
videos.description:
Since the mid-2010s, Karkhana’s seven multi-instrumentalists have been making music that is rooted in oriental traditions and continually grows in the direction of the future The group’s members have lived around the globe: from Egypt, Lebanon and Turkey to Germany all the way to North America. Yet Karkhana’s musical home is in the spiritual realm. Where elements of the Lebanese Oud tradition meet Egyptian Shaabi, the spirituality of Sufism, Jazz and Kraut (without the rock, according to the band themselves) in order to create a “free music of the Middle East”.

Karkhana - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021

videos.title: Max Andrzejewski "Mythos I - eio" - live @ Bonello Studio | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
„Mythos I - eio“ was commissioned by Berliner Ensemble and Ersan Mondtag as one of four ouvertures for "-w̶̶a̶̶g̶̶n̶̶e̶̶r̶̶ ̶–̶ ̶̶d̶̶e̶̶r̶̶ ̶̶r̶̶i̶̶n̶̶g̶̶ ̶̶d̶̶e̶̶s̶̶ ̶̶n̶̶i̶̶b̶̶e̶̶l̶̶u̶̶n̶̶g̶̶e̶̶n̶ (a piece like fresh chopped eschenwood)" recomposed by Thomas Köck. The piece premiered on June 3rd 2021 at Berliner Ensemble.

Andrzejewski’s music has nothing to do with Wagner‘s originals in the outcome, it‘s rather a counter-draft. However, small material from Wagner‘s overtures were chopped up and decontextualised and then used as composition material, or for the electronic elements in the pieces.

Max Andrzejewski "Mythos I - eio" - live @ Bonello Studio | 2021
FILMED BY EAR is a series by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: inEvitable feat. Hayden Chisholm "When a woman drinks" @ Studio Börne | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Researching the balance in the coexistence of composition and improvisation, precision and authentic personal expression, contemporary music, jazz and greek folk music, greek percussionist Evi Filippou formed the quintet in.Evi.table. Greek traditional rhythms, rembetiko melodies from the ’20s and reharmonisations of beloved folk songs flirt with the experimental sound of Berlin’s jazz scene. The listener enters a microcosmos which resembles at the same time a cabaret, a contemporary music concert, a greek feast and New York in the ’60s as Evi imagines it.

inEvitable feat. Hayden Chisholm "When a woman drinks" - live @ Studio Börne | 2021
LIVE IN BERLIN is a series by @berta.berlin.

Text: Pythagoras

videos.title: Pheromone Poetry "Lacerta Cluster" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
Led by musical pheromones, the path that leads through “Lacerta Cluster” is quite intricate and convoluted: Beginning with a hymn dedicated to our skeleton, it’s followed by a friendship song, the sound of three flutes – one of which copulates with a clarinet – and a noble composition that beguiles the note D. Moving on, it leads past the erotic allure of a saxophone wrestling with the New York School’s enthusiastic lack of intention, to finally culminate in a levitating free jazz quartet.

Pheromone Poetry "Lacerta Cluster" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
Filmed by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Lapsus Lumine "Moondog Project" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! Festival 2021
videos.description:
When Louis Thomas Hardin died in Münster in 1999, the world lost a true visionary. But the legend that he created lives on: that of his alter ego Moondog, a blind Viking who stood on New York street corners and, in connection with primal forces, dreamt up an otherworldly music that has left a lasting impact on jazz greats and minimalists.

The Moondog Project was created to pay homage to this legend. Together with Ernst Reijseger on the cello and Jim Black on the drums, the vocalists Marta del Grandi, Sabrina Oggero Viale and Erika Sofia Sollo as well as bassist Stefano Risso (the four of whom are also known as Lapsus Lumine) reinterpret Moondog’s works and also present their own compositions that have been inspired by his aesthetic. A loving tribute. The legend lives on.

Lapsus Lumine - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.
Watch all concert films here: https://www.alarmefestival.de/concert-films-alarme

videos.title: Interstellar Waggle Swarm "Interstellar Waggle Crush" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
A suite composed by Gregoire Simon, “Interstellar Waggle Swarm” sees various melodies and polyrhythmic structures join for a dance – thus re-arranging and forming ever new swarm-like constellations and musical textures. The term “waggle dance” refers to the honey bee’s figure-eight dance that sees them shake and move in order to share information with the rest of the colony. Just like bees when a storm is about to erupt, the 17 ensemble members remain in tight-knit/constant vibration with each other, as they get carried away in trance-like states, fight the ensuing chaos and always find clever ways to converge wherever the tide is taking them…

Interstellar Waggle Swarm "Interstellar Waggle Crush" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
 Filmed by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Zbigniew Chojnacki - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! FESTIVAL 2021
videos.description:
According to Zbigniew Chojnacki, he can be inspired by a washing machine or an empty chair in the middle of a stage. Or an old accordion, surrounded by effect pedals, loop machines and other landmines of sound.

This is the set-up that the Polish musician currently uses to generate new ideas and truly unexpected sounds. Often dismissed as a sort of “folklore jukebox”, Pauline Oliveros has already demonstrated the possibilities hidden within the humble accordion. In Chojnacki’s hands, the instrument is in a permanent state of metamorphosis. At one point, it seems to be a cross between a typewriter and a bellows. At first meditative like an endless drone, then exhilarating like a washing machine drum during a spin cycle.

Zbigniew Chojnacki - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
A series by @berta.berlin and A L'ARME! Festival.

Performer
Zbigniew Chojnacki — accordion, electronics

videos.title: Farida Amadou & Julien Desprez - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | A L'ARME! FESTIVAL 2021
videos.description:
“Ten Steel Strings for a Hallelujah”. In the main roles: bass, guitar and electricity.

Their approach is as pure and unadulterated as a meeting of two strong musical personalities can be: their focus lies in communication, in approaching and touching, seizing and contrasting, in agreeing and crying out as one. For the audience, this dialogue becomes a cleansing ritual for the ears, the body and the soul.

Farida Amadou & Julien Desprez - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021

videos.description:
Justus Raym's musical philosophy follows a certain set of rules or mantras, which he describes as "movement = sanity, static=insanity".
It is these phrases that are the inspiration behind the conception and drive of his work. Justus explores very different sides of his personality over beautiful, complex and enchanted musical pieces. Here he shares his honest feelings and intimate experiences.

videos.title: Cosmic Swarm Laboratory “Cassiopeia Cloud” - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
The term “doldrums” essentially means “lull” or “dead calm” – and that’s exactly what happens in the most elegant way as “Doldrums deluxe” unfolds. Sleazy emotions are frozen in mid-air, as a beautiful nightmare of stasis and utter inertia takes over: despite the greatest efforts to shake things up, the musicians and the machine meet at a complete standstill.

Cosmic Swarm Laboratory “Cassiopeia Cloud” - live @ Radialsystem | 2021
 Filmed by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Pheromone Poetry "Perseus Flock" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
The “Perseus Flock” comprises countless strings of all lengths and sizes surrounding a flute. Initially following New Age paths, it soon gets caught up in a thick undergrowth of blues quintet/classical roots, then looks for further possible pastures in traditional Czech folk terrain and eventually arrives on a musical plateau that links all those different spheres – finally at peace with itself and its contradictions.

Pheromone Poetry "Perseus Flock" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
 Filmed by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Max Andrzejewski "Mythos II - aüe" - live @ Bonello Studio | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Max Andrzejewski "Mythos II - aüe" - live @ Bonello Studio | 2021

„Mythos II - aüe“ was commissioned by Berliner Ensemble and Ersan Mondtag as one of four ouvertures for "-w̶̶a̶̶g̶̶n̶̶e̶̶r̶̶ ̶–̶ ̶̶d̶̶e̶̶r̶̶ ̶̶r̶̶i̶̶n̶̶g̶̶ ̶̶d̶̶e̶̶s̶̶ ̶̶n̶̶i̶̶b̶̶e̶̶l̶̶u̶̶n̶̶g̶̶e̶̶n̶ (a piece like fresh chopped eschenwood)" recomposed by Thomas Köck. The piece premiered on June 3rd 2021 at Berliner Ensemble.

Andrzejewski’s music has nothing to do with Wagner‘s originals in the outcome, it‘s rather a counter-draft. However, small material from Wagner‘s overtures were chopped up and decontextualised and then used as composition material, or for the electronic elements in the pieces.

The music is played by a bunch of great musicians from the classical and improvising field.

videos.title: Cosmic Swarm Laboratory "Lacerta Cluster" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | KOSMOSTAGE IV
videos.description:
“Likolit” is the name of an abandoned linoleum factory that towers over the surrounding roofs of Kohlmühle, a tiny village situated in the middle of the magical landscape of the Elbe Sandstone Mountains (between Saxony and Bohemia). The eponymous composition, inspired by the old plant and written by Johannes Schleiermacher, plays with the relationship between individual vs. swarm, thus creating a framework within the “Cosmic Swarm Laboratory” where musicians, algorithm and sonographer can interact.

Cosmic Swarm Laboratory "Lacerta Cluster" - live @ Radialsystem, Berlin | 2021
 Filmed by @berta.berlin.

videos.title: Live-Talk Rangarang Chor | KLANGTEPPICH
videos.description:
Fragen und Kommentare sind willkommen, diskutieren Sie mit!

Mit Franziska Buhre (Kuratorin Klangteppich)
Sophie Grobler, Pejman Jamilpanah, Pari (Sänger_innen)
Laura Winkler (Arrangements, Chorleitung)
Ehrengast Ali Kamrani

Moderation: Susan Zare

Laura Winkler war 2016 mit ihrer Band Holler My Dear zu Gast in Iran. 2018 entstand die Idee für einen Chor aus persischen und deutschen Muttersprachler_innen, die gemeinsam Lieder der bekannten Sängerinnen Hayedeh (1942-1990) Marzieh (1924-2010) und Ramesh (1946-2020) singen. Für den Rangarang Chor zelebriert Laura Winkler diese Sängerinnen mit neuen Arrangements einiger Lieder.

Ali Kamrani, geboren 1952 in Teheran, ist Dichter und Schauspieler. Im Alter von 20 Jahren begann er, Gedichte und Lieder zu schreiben, u.a. für die Sängerin Neli. Seit 1979 lebt Kamrani in Deutschland.

Warum bewegt uns die Popmusik Irans aus den 1960/70er noch heute? Und wie können wir die Lebenswege der Sängerinnen in Iran und im Exil heute einordnen?

Die Teilnehmenden sprechen über Teilhabe von Frauen am Musikschaffen in Iran und Ländern des globalen Nordens, Kunstfreiheit und die gemeinsame Musikpraxis über Sprach- und Geschlechtergrenzen hinweg.

Das Chorprojekt im Rahmen des Festivals Klangteppich III wird gefördert von der Bundeszentrale für politische Bildung.

videos.title: Festival video premiere | KLANGTEPPICH
videos.description:
With 4 world premieres!

00:00:00 Hadi Bastani [artist in residence] – modular synthesizer
Yalda Zamani – electronic sounds, codes, motion-sensors
Bastani works with field recordings, computers, modular synthesizers, transducers, and hacked electronic toys in the context of electroacoustic compositions, improvised performances, and site-specific, immersive and interactive installations. Zamani works with live coding and the application of machine learning models in combination with motion-sensing technologies.

00:30:58 RCO
Karl Heinz Jeron
As Klangteppich’s guests* of Noiselab festival, RCO realize an ephemeral, improvised and multi-layered sound installation with electronic circuits, wired everyday objects and different gadgets.
Noiselab is dedicated to experimental and electronic music at the interface of contemporary sound art and offers a common framework for improvisational musicians, sound tinkerers, electronic inventors and performance artists who want to present their non-commercial art and sound practice to a broad public and develop it collectively.

01:08:43 XEEN – electronics
Orlando Boeykens – tuba
Guitarist and producer Zhina Ardalan founded her solo project Xeen in 2019, and Orlando Boeykens is a sought-after tuba player in orchestras, chamber ensembles and bands. Xeen was curious to combine her sounds with those of the tuba, Boeykens was directly involved. Their performance celebrates noise and microtonality, reduction and opulence.

01:47:45 Rangarang Choir
Laura Winkler – arrangements, direction
Winnie Brückner, Omid Darvish, Sophie Grobler, Pejman Jamilpanah, Zola Mennenöh, Pari – vocals
The composer and singer Laura Winkler arranges songs by the popular Iranian singers Hayedeh (1942-1990) Marzieh (1924-2010) and Ramesh (1946-2020) for a choir of Persian native speakers and singers who do not speak Persian. This project was made possible through funding by the Federal Agency for Civic Education.

videos.title: LORD OF THE AMAZING PANTHER - live @ Club Gretchen | LIVING IN A BOX
videos.description:
‘Lord of The Amazing Panther’ / LOTAP are something like steroid cougars, a real insider tip supergroup straight outta Berlin. Each member is a monster of work equipment and together they are probably the hottest groove package in the capital.
With this project Schlippenbach (DJ Illvibe) in particular shows his own musical vision for the first time. They play with unconditional devotion to beats, improvisation and always with precise sound fetishism. The Panther jumps far and always unpredictably, lightly uses a wide variety of influences and plays a highly independent musical cosmos in an absolutely unmistakable and uncompromising way. 
„Lord of The Amazing Panther are the answer to everything” - DJ Koze

Lord of The Amazing Panther - live @ Club Gretchen | 2020
LIVING IN A BOX is a series by @berta.berlin & @clubGRETCHEN .

videos.title: "Lisa" by Max Andrzejewski | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
This piece by Max Andrzejewski was commissioned by Podium Festival Esslingen as a part of the hourlong composition „Zauberburg“ for 9 musicians, electronics and choir, premiered in October 2020. „Lisa“ is based on a medical questionnaire of the singer Lisa Ströckens. The text copes with the material value of the chemical elements in the human body.

Performer
Lisa Ströckens - soprano
Ragnar Jónsson - cello
Maria Schneider - percussion
Moritz Ter Nedden - violin, crotales

videos.title: Kid Be Kid - live @ Theater im Delphi | FILMED BY EAR
videos.description:
Kid Be Kid - live @ Theater im Delphi | 2020

A unique skill set of beatboxing, vocals, piano and synthesizer meets poetic lyrics and rhythmic virtuosity. Before she appeared on the scene, it was hard to imagine an artist playing four instruments simultaneously without a loop station, grooving and touching to the max and splitting her voice into several tones. She embodies coolness and vulnerability, structure and freedom, hip hop and jazz equally and makes everything in the room come alive.

videos.title: FROLLEIN SMILLA are LIVING IN A YARD | live at Club Gretchen
videos.description:
Frollein Smilla loves variety, in terms of style, instruments, even language. She loves the variation, and there's no lack of changes in tempo, dynamics, harmony and rhythm either. What she doesn't like is reduction to a common denominator. The concerts also celebrate the high art of musicality. Piano, accordion and multilayered horns, sensitive and energetic percussion, acoustic and electric guitars and basses are brought richly to life with great feeling and even greater skill. All this is crowned by the mesmerising, hearty harmonies of the polyphonic vocals in English, German and Spanish.

▶️ https://berta.berlin/frollein-smilla-2

Performer
Desna Wackerhagen – vocals, guitar
Anja Kitzing –  vocals, keys, accordion

videos.description:
Urban music with dark beats, dazzling synths and pulsating hooks. Multilayered orchestral arrangements are contrasted with a frank and translucent voice that cuts through the clutter. The result is a phantasmagorical world that mirrors our contemporary state as an absurd, but wonderful combination of natural and virtual elements.

videos.description:
Ernstablecht Stiebler is a composer of minimalist and reductionist contemporary music. Stiebler's approach is best summarized in his own words: "Music is too important to burden it with emotions." Together with musician, stage director and curator Tilman Kanitz he explores the possibilities of an inner perception of sound and strange sound spaces.

videos.title: Stella Chiweshe - live @ Club Gretchen | LIVING IN A BOX
videos.description:
Stella Chiweshe - live @ Club Gretchen | 2020

The queen of Mbira music from Zimbabwe - like Stella Chiweshe is affectionately called, is the first female artist who gained prestige in a music tradition that's been dominated by men: Mbira music. A breathtakingly powerful and highly spiritual performamce of one of the most original artists in the contemporary African scene using popular music to show the deepness and power of traditional spiritual music at home and abroad.

videos.title: Spoiler at Jazzwoche / Sowieso | live in Berlin
videos.description:
Harsh cuts, sharp turns and unremitting, longing slow-burns - the quartet manoeuvres restlessly from detailed textured intricacies to anthemic hooks over laid-back grooves, tugging you mercilessly into condensed, punishing passages of reckless thrashing that dissipate instantaneously into a sparse, whispering serenity, or a grounding, riff-y, cyclical vortex. A landscape of blooming, fleeting architectures.

Performer:
Brad Henkel - trumpet, electronics
Liz Kosack - synthesizers
Julia Reidy - guitar
Samuel Hall - drums

videos.title: FROLLEIN SMILLA is LIVING IN A BOX #12 | live at Club Gretchen
videos.description:
Frollein Smilla loves variety, in terms of style, instruments, even language. She loves the variation, and there's no lack of changes in tempo, dynamics, harmony and rhythm either. What she doesn't like is reduction to a common denominator. The concerts also celebrate the high art of musicality. Piano, accordion and multilayered horns, sensitive and energetic percussion, acoustic and electric guitars and basses are brought richly to life with great feeling and even greater skill. All this is crowned by the mesmerising, hearty harmonies of the polyphonic vocals in English, German and Spanish.

▶️ https://berta.berlin/frollein-smilla

videos.description:
Yusuf Sahilli might sound like a blues man, or a new romantic, growling about wild nights spent searching, or letting his sweet tenor voice indulge in lustful melancholy moods. Fully buzzed on global sounds and brimming with boundless feel good passion and sympathy for the whole wide world. Yusuf had connected to the Alternative Rock soundtrack of his generation and dug deep and fast into the history of the trade. From the Rock dinosaurs and the golden age of Pop through the Hippie era, the Beat of the Sixties and the protest movement right down to the founding fathers of the Blues in the Delta.

videos.title: WITOLD (Kalle Kalima / Uli Kempendorff / Moritz Baumgärtner) - live @ Donau115 | LIVE IN BERLIN
videos.description:
WITOLD (Kalle Kalima / Uli Kempendorff / Moritz Baumgärtner) - live @ Donau115 | 2020

The mixture of naive melodies, crooked folk forms and Lutoslawskis partly wonderful, partly cryptic and dense harmonies, offers exactly the right starting material for improvisation and trio-conversations with an invisible fourth man.

🔔 Subscribe to berta.berlin & hit the bell to get notified about new performances:

videos.title: Els Vandeweyer @ West Germany | LIVE FROM BERLIN
videos.description:
Els Vandeweyer is a Belgian vibraphonist, percussionist, and composer. After studying classical percussion in Antwerp and jazz vibraphone in Brussels and Oslo, Els co-founded the IMI Kollektief. Els plays regularly in Berlin, including solo performance, group improvisation, and contemporary music
